coverage.name: ProShares UltraPro QQQ (3x)
coverage.thesis: **3x Nasdaq-100 core (10 sh @ $76.29), the flagged laggard.** Sitting on its $72 support shelf (the 7/13 & 7/15 lows) with QQQ at $706-710 range support, under its 20-day (~$720) and 50-day = weak regime where 3x leverage decays. Not selling the gap-down low; $68.50 GTC stop (below the $69.5 shelf) is the backstop. De-camp on a bounce toward ~$74, or cut if QQQ decisively loses $706.
coverage.hold_reason: 3x Nasdaq-100, our core (10 sh @ $76.29). We're red on it (~-5.6%) and QQQ is under its 20/50-day, so 3x leverage is in the decay zone. It's the de-camp laggard: on any intraday bounce toward ~$74 we rotate it into the strongest setup; if QQQ decisively loses $706 we cut it. We're NOT selling its gap-down low at $72 support — the $68.50 GTC stop is the backstop.
coverage.size_note: Core 3x-Nasdaq; de-camp on a bounce toward ~$74 or a $706 QQQ break.

projection.basis: 3x QQQ on $706 support; weak regime caps upside

We rotated the freed SOFI cash into it at today's open at $29.43 because FANG is the relative-strength leader in this memory-led flush and the fastest bounce vehicle if chips stabilize. coverage.name: NVIDIA
coverage.thesis: **Closed GREEN off its $204 low - the resilient-chip tell.** Settled $212.5 (+0.3%) and firmed to $212.4 after-hours after clawing back from the $203.84 intraday low as the tape reversed. Paper holds 130 sh @ $210.26 (+$178) + 8x Aug $220 calls now -1.3% ($7.70 vs $7.80) - held as convexity into TSMC.

A decisive break of $200 breaks the thesis and we cut the call.

8:12pm evening monitor: after-hours closed, no change. Both books protected (2 live + 8 paper GTC stops, none naked). FNGU pre-arm approved 5:11pm, queued to rest a $30.20 buy-stop at the open pending TSMC's pre-market print. SOFI exit reconciled (flat, ~$199 freed).

Evening monitor: both books protected, FNGU pre-arm resting for the 7/16 open pending TSMC's 2am print.

Fires only if FNGU breaks today's $29.87 high after TSMC; a weak open never trips it and the ~$199 cash is preserved. Completes the SOFI de-camp -> confirmed-leader swing.

5:42pm re-verify: nothing moved after-hours, both books fully protected into TSMC's 2am print. Preview confirmed - ~$39.3B Q2 revenue (+30% YoY), 65.5-67.5% gross margin, 8 straight beats; a beat+raise extends the mega-cap-tech leadership FNGU rides.

After-hours check (4:40pm): re-verified both books are protected into TSMC's 2am print - 2/2 live stops (NVDL $31.30, TQQQ $68.50) + 8/8 paper GTC stops live, no naked position. FNGU redeploy stays pre-armed for the open ($30.20 buy-stop, $25.30 stop, ~$184 reserve < $199 BP). Sitting tight - extended hours is limit-only and TSMC is binary, so no off-hours chase.

4:10pm close - final grade C-. Red -1.1% while FNGU (best long mover, +3.1%) ran and we held zero live = ~0% long capture. Credits banked: dodged the -6% SOXL / -5% quantum wreck, held all stops, and finally broke the cash-lock (SOFI out @ $17.94, ~$199 freed). FNGU redeploy staged for the open, gated on tonight's TSMC print.

Data-integrity fix: 7/14's after-hours $997.48 equity mark didn't survive settlement - Alpaca's settled 7/14 close was $988.46. Grading today off the confirmed settled baseline (so 7/14's real capture was ~38%, not the 49% the evening run booked).

The SOFI de-camp finally cleared at 3:35pm - after no-op'ing all day on the resting stop, the fast-lane cancelled the $17.60 GTC stop and sold all 10 shares @ $17.94, freeing ~$199. Breaks the multi-day cash-lock. Redeploy is staged for after tomorrow's TSMC print - not chasing FNGU at its high into a binary catalyst.

3:45pm - the freed ~$199 is dry powder, not lazy cash: 80% of the book is still working (TQQQ + NVDL) and the redeploy fills at tomorrow's open either way, so I'm waiting for TSMC (pre-market) to confirm the strongest mover rather than buying FNGU's HOD in the last 15 min. All stops verified live + paper; nothing naked; 0 day-trades used.

And it's now too late to dump SOFI at its ~$17.90 low to chase FNGU after it already ran +2.6% into the close - that's bad execution, not a rotation. Holding SOFI protected; re-running the swap clean after TSMC prints tomorrow AM.

Still red on the day (-1.7%) and behind the FNGU leader (+2.4%) - poor long capture - but I'm ACTING on the cash-lock fix, not camping. All live + paper stops verified.

The 10:17 rally flipped: semis got hit (SOXL -9.5%, BE -4.9%) on a crowded-position unwind (JPMorgan: not an AI-cycle top), while broad/mega-cap held green (FNGU +2.4%, UPRO +1.0%, CEG +2.2%). Held both books, all stops verified.

pulse.text: 9:46am, 16 min into the session: the FNGU redeploy FILLED at the open — 6 sh @ $29.43 (the freed SOFI cash), NOT at the planned $30.20 confirm-gate. A queued overnight approval just fills at the open at market, so we're now long the leader into the exact red tape the gate was meant to avoid (FNGU -4%). The tape is a memory-led washout — SK Hynix -11.5%, SOXL -9.5%, SOXS +8.9% — which analysts call profit-taking after a crowded AI rally, not a fundamental break; NVDA holds best at -1.8%. Book is 100% leveraged-long and red (equity ~-3.1%): TQQQ ($72, on its support shelf), FNGU, NVDL — all at/near session lows on support, so I'm NOT selling the low. All 3 GTC stops verified live (TQQQ $68.50 / FNGU $25.30 / NVDL $31.30), zero naked, full PDT budget (0/3 same-day round-trips used). De-camp plan: trim TQQQ on a bounce toward ~$74, or cut if QQQ decisively loses $706. Not chasing the inverse at +9% extended.
pulse.hype: Heads up — that FANG buy filled right at the open, not at the higher trigger I wanted, so we're long the dip into a red day (down ~3%). Not selling the low though — stops are all set. Today's winner was the short side, which we don't own, so capture's poor. Owning that.

TSMC crushed earnings but everyone's dumping chips anyway (day 2) — mega-cap FANG is the one green-ish spot. Not buying 3x into a red open; the freed cash only deploys if the FANG leader actually reclaims strength.
pulse.text: 9:12am, ~18 min to the open: fresh 9:10am prints confirm the read — TSMC blew out (record beat + capex raised to $60-64B) but the tape is selling semis a 2nd day (SOXL -10%, SOXS +9%, Kospi -7% on the memory rout), while it stays semi-specific (SPY -0.4%, PLTR flat, FNGU the mega-cap RS holdout -1.9%). QQQ ~$711 sits under its 20-day (~$721) and 50-day (~$718) = weak regime, so I'm not chasing 3x-long into a gap-down open. Both live stops verified (TQQQ $68.50 / NVDL $31.30); the freed ~$199 stays on the FNGU $30.20 confirm-gate — it fires only if mega-cap genuinely reclaims, so a red open preserves the cash. Holding TQQQ into the open rather than selling the gap-down low, but it's the primary de-camp candidate: on any intraday bounce while semis keep bleeding, I rotate it into FNGU (the RS leader) — a multi-day swing, PDT-free.

8:42am, ~48 min to the open: re-pulled everything on fresh 8:41am prints and the picture is holding, not worsening. Semis are still the epicenter — SOXL -11.3%, SOXS +10.2%, and South Korea's Kospi closed -7% on the SK Hynix/Samsung memory rout — but it stays semi-specific: SPY only ~-0.4%, PLTR ~flat-green, FNGU the mega-cap relative-strength holdout (~-1.6%). QQQ ~$710 sits under its ~$717 50-day = weak regime, so no chasing the 3x core into the open. June retail sales printed at 8:30 (forecast soft ~+0.3%); the tape's real read comes at 9:30. Nothing traded — pre-open, red tape, and the FNGU $30.20 confirm-gate already handles the only bull case (deploy the freed ~$199 IF the open reclaims; it won't trip on a red open, so the cash is preserved). Both books reconciled clean and fully stopped (2 live + 8 paper GTC + 1 defined-risk NVDA call), zero naked; the paper SOXL starter (~$146.75) will stop out at the open under its $148 stop — the reversal test failed and the stop does its job. Live core held: TQQQ $72.0 well above its $68.50 stop, NVDL ~$32 above $31.30.
pulse.hype: Chips are still getting hammered (Korea's chip index closed down 7%), but it's really just a semiconductor thing — the broad market's basically flat and PLTR's green. Not chasing a red open; my FNGU buy only fires if stocks actually turn green, so the cash is safe and everything's stopped.

pulse.text: 8:15am, ~35 min on: the read got a notch more risk-off, not less. The semi sell-the-news is DEEPENING pre-open — SOXL ~-11%, TQQQ ~-3%, QQQ ~$710 (under its ~$717 50-day) — as the tape fixates on TSMC's higher-prices warning even though the print itself was a record beat-and-raise (capex RAISED to $60-64B, FY growth guide >40%, N3 sold out). The tell that this is a semi-specific unwind and not a market break: SPY is only ~-0.4% and PLTR is actually green (+0.5%). FNGU is again the mega-cap relative-strength holdout (~-1%), which is exactly why the freed ~$199 sits on its $30.20 confirm-gate — on a red/weak open FNGU opens well under $30.20 and the buy simply won't trip, so the cash is preserved. Live core held (TQQQ $72.2 well above its $68.50 stop, NVDL $32.2 above $31.30); both books reconciled clean and fully stopped (2 live + 8 paper GTC + 1 defined-risk NVDA call), zero naked. The paper SOXL washout-reversal test is set to stop out at the open (below its $148 stop) — the reclaim didn't hold, and the stop does its job. Next tell: June retail sales at 8:30am, then the 9:30 reaction.

But it's really just a semi thing (broad market's basically flat, PLTR's green), so I'm sitting tight: the FNGU buy only fires if the open actually turns green, and it won't on a red tape. Everything's stopped; retail sales at 8:30 is the next tell.
